long_stay: Full-time bachelor's, master's, or doctoral programmes
Full-time vocational training at recognised institutions
Short-term exchange programs lasting more than 90 days

types.name: Long-stay Study Visa
types.notes: Single-entry visa, valid for entry into Hungary to collect residence permit.

booking_methods.description: Official online appointment booking system for Hungarian consulates.
Third-party visa application centers used in some countries; check the Hungarian embassy website for your specific country.

steps.title: Find the Hungarian Embassy or Consulate
steps.description: Locate the Hungarian Embassy or Consulate in your country of citizenship or legal residence that holds jurisdiction.

steps.title: Book an Appointment
steps.description: Book an appointment online via the Konzinfo booking system or VFS Global portal. It is recommended to book at least 2 months before your intended departure.

steps.title: Submit Application In Person
steps.description: Submit all required documents in person at the consular office, where biometric data (photo and fingerprints) will be captured. Bring your appointment confirmation.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: At least 2 years validity recommended to avoid delays.

required_documents.name: Passport-sized Photos
required_documents.details: Two recent photos, 3.5 × 4.5 cm, white background, not older than 3 months.

required_documents.name: University Acceptance Letter
required_documents.details: Official letter of acceptance from the Hungarian educational institution.

required_documents.name: Proof of Tuition Fee Payment
required_documents.details: Bank/wire transfer receipt + university confirmation letter, or scholarship certificate.

required_documents.name: Proof of Accommodation
required_documents.details: University dormitory confirmation or signed rental lease agreement. For private flats, rental agreement must be signed by two witnesses and property registration document (tulajdoni lap) issued within 30 days.

required_documents.name: Health Insurance
required_documents.details: Valid in Hungary, covering at least €30,000 across the Schengen area for the entire planned stay. Costing about €150-€300 per year.

required_documents.name: Proof of Financial Means
required_documents.details: 6 months certified bank statements (student or parent/guardian), scholarship award letter, or notarized affidavit of support from a financial sponsor with their employment records and bank statements. Proof of money origin and family ties may be required.

required_documents.name: Clean Criminal Record Certificate
required_documents.details: Not a universal requirement for all nationalities; check specific embassy guidelines.

required_documents.name: Language Proficiency Certificate
required_documents.details: Certificate or official diploma proving study language of previous degree.

financial_requirements.reference_basis: Sufficient funds to cover tuition, accommodation, health insurance, and living expenses for the entire stay.
acceptable_proof: Certified bank statements (past 3-6 months) from student or parent/guardian
Notarized affidavit of support/commitment letter from a financial sponsor
Sponsor's employment records and bank statements
Proof of money origin and family ties
exemptions: Scholarship holders (if scholarship covers all living expenses)

required_documents: Proof of accommodation for Address Card (lakcímkártya)
residence_permit.notes: For Type D visa holders, the residence permit is delivered directly to the educational institution. Do not apply online via EnterHungary after arrival, as the application was already submitted at the consulate. Do not visit OIF in person to collect the permit.

restrictions: Maximum 90 days or 66 working days per year outside the study period.
work_while_studying.notes: Employer must register the student, and student must obtain a Hungarian Tax ID (adóazonosító jel) before starting work.

required_documents: Current transcripts showing satisfactory academic progress
permit_renewal.notes: Renewal can only be done in Hungary. Do not travel abroad without the new or extended permit, as a new D-type visa application would be required. If changing educational institution, a new extension application with the new admission certificate is required via EnterHungary.

requirements: Must have graduated from a Hungarian university or college.
Apply for the 'Job-Seeking Residence Permit' no later than 15 days before your student residence permit expires.
Secure a job offer or register a business within the 9-month job-seeking period.
Convert to a residence permit for employment, EU Blue Card, or Hungary Card upon securing employment.
post_study_work.notes: This interim permit allows graduates to stay and seek employment or start a business after graduation.

official_sources.name: OIF (National Directorate-General for Aliens Policing)
official_sources.description: Official guidelines, forms, and digital submissions.

official_sources.name: Study in Hungary (official)
official_sources.description: General student support and official scholarship information.

tips.title: Verify Current Requirements
tips.body: Hungarian immigration rules change rapidly. Always verify current requirements directly with the OIF (https://oif.gov.hu) or the Hungarian Embassy in your country before applying.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying for a visa too late
common_mistakes.consequence: Risk of visa refusal or not receiving visa before course start.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Start your visa application at least 90 days before your course begins, but not earlier than 3 months before the planned start date.

common_mistakes.mistake: Insufficient or incorrect proof of funds
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure you can prove sufficient funds to cover all expenses (tuition, accommodation, health, living) with clear, certified bank documents from the past 3-6 months, or formal sponsorship.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying for a visa from within Hungary (for Type D visa holders)
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Applications must be submitted from your home country at the Hungarian Embassy or Consulate.

common_mistakes.mistake: Waiting too long to apply for or collect the residence permit after arrival
common_mistakes.consequence: Invalidation of legal stay, potential fines, or delays in obtaining the permit.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Register your accommodation and ensure your residence permit is processed within 30 days of arrival. For Type D visa holders, the permit is delivered to the university.

common_mistakes.consequence: Potential issues with residence permit validity.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Submit a new extension application via EnterHungary with your new admission certificate.

common_mistakes.mistake: Missing the study-to-work permit application deadline
common_mistakes.consequence: Loss of eligibility for the 9-month job-seeking period.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply for the 'Job-Seeking Residence Permit' at least 15 days before your student residence permit expires.

common_mistakes.consequence: Extra bookings will be cancelled without notice.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: One person may book only one appointment for a consular service.

common_mistakes.mistake: Incomplete or invalid health insurance documentation
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Make sure your policy explicitly covers Hungary and the full duration of your stay, with at least €30,000 coverage across the Schengen area.

common_mistakes.mistake: Incomplete or incorrect rental documents for private accommodation
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Your rental agreement must be signed by two witnesses, and you must submit the property registration document (tulajdoni lap) issued within the past 30 days.

common_mistakes.mistake: Unexplained large cash deposits in bank statements
common_mistakes.consequence: Funds may be deemed artificial, leading to visa refusal.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Keep funds stable in your account for months or rely heavily on formal parental sponsorship documentation.
